vidyut-realize.note: Real Vidyut-realized corpus pulled from workstream/vidyut-realize; targets computed directly from each line's surface text (no proxy, no cached Saṃsādhanī blob).

D-bracket-valid.note: 100000 sequences from the matched config (n_shuffles=1 -> pure Dyck); 0 invalid under is_shuffle_valid + is_balanced + depth bound.

D-match-eps.check: best matched config within eps of REAL vidyut sentence-level targets on DEFAULT_KEYS
D-match-eps.note: Targets TTR=0.011589, bigram_H=0.911361, trigram_H=0.986538 computed from 10k realized sentences. Matched config {bracket_types:35, max_depth:16, n_shuffles:1, min_len:8, max_len:96}. Reproduced within eps by an independent regeneration in test_dyck_match_real.

D-hu-pin.check: hu_replication_config fields match ADR-0025 Hu pin (k cap per ASCII alphabet)
D-hu-pin.note: TestHuReplicationConfig pins all fields; reported separately from the H1 match (Hu distance to real targets = 0.0879).

D-standalone.check: worktree tests run standalone (no torch / Saṃsādhanī import), GPU-runnable
D-standalone.note: Domain modules import torch-free (asserted in TestStandalone); corpus generation is deterministic CPU/GPU-agnostic Python (RNG-seeded), runs identically on GB10.

byte_length_note: Whitespace-token byte-length L1 vs real corpus = 1.69 and is reported informational-only (byte_hist_is_gated=false): Dyck uses atomic single-byte symbols while Sanskrit uses multi-byte words, making this cross-tokenization comparison degenerate. Post-tokenization length parity is owned by the measurement workstream's shared tokenizer.
gate: GATE 2 (per-workstream standalone closure); awaiting human sign-off
